projects/job-9568.label: Schwartz cosmetics booth
projects/job-9568.represents: Schwartz Natural Cosmetics booth at a cosmetics trade show, showing private-label skincare and haircare lines on branded shelving plus logo and cut-tree close-ups.
projects/job-9568.structures: Modular corner booth in whitewashed shiplap-print panels with a slatted pergola ceiling, dark-teal floating shelves and three teal pedestal display cubes. A large laser-cut tree silhouette on the right is confirmed cardboard X-Board by image 8, which shows the brown corrugated fluted cut edge. Backlit printed header panels for Tamar, Superfood and Private Label and a round illuminated Schwartz logo disc are wall-mounted.
projects/job-9568.branding: SCHWARTZ NATURAL COSMETICS, Since 1965, with a hand-drawn tree icon on a round disc and the counter. Sub-brands TAMAR for Mediterranean essence, SUPERFOOD for prickly pear, PRIVATE LABEL for pomegranate, plus LYCHEE. Palette is whitewashed wood with dark teal accents and red and yellow product pops.
projects/job-9568.setting: Indoor expo hall with black industrial ceiling, structural columns and track lighting on dark flooring. Neighboring COSMETICS AND PERSONAL CARE signage confirms a cosmetics-industry exhibition. Small open corner stand with a meeting table and clear Ghost-style acrylic chairs.
projects/job-9568.reuse: Strong portfolio piece. Images 4, 5 and 6 are best wide hero shots, images 1, 2 and 3 are product-wall thumbnails, image 8 is an excellent X-Board build-detail proof shot, and image 7 is a clean logo crop. No re-shoot needed, only minor glare on glossy panels.

projects/kraft-utility-shelf.label: Kraft Utility Shelf
projects/kraft-utility-shelf.branding: None. Raw natural kraft-brown cardboard, blank surfaces, no logos, color, or text.
projects/kraft-utility-shelf.represents: Multi-angle studio product shots of one modular utility shelving unit made entirely from thick honeycomb-core kraft cardboard (X-Board). Tall vertical panels stand in a folded zig-zag plan with cantilevered shelves slotted through them at four to five levels, forming a self-standing free-edge display/bookcase fixture.
projects/kraft-utility-shelf.reuse: Good catalog/thumbnail and secondary-hero material for an X-Board furniture/retail-fixture category; clean, consistent multi-angle set that shows the honeycomb build and modularity well. Limits: studio-only, no event context, branding, or human scale, plus minor room artifacts (outlet, baseboard). Use front frames (Kraft-utility-shelf.jpg, _4) as thumbnails and angled frames (_1, _2, _3, _5) as detail shots; re-shoot only if a styled, in-use, or event-context hero is needed.
projects/kraft-utility-shelf.setting: Indoor plain white studio room: seamless near-white floor and walls, soft even lighting. Some frames show a baseboard, wall corner, and an electrical outlet, indicating a real white room rather than an infinity cove. Not an event or expo floor.
projects/kraft-utility-shelf.structures: Self-standing modular shelving in heavy honeycomb-core cardboard (~25-35mm). Rounded-top vertical panels in a folded zig-zag plan; flat shelves insert through slots and cantilever out, some rectangular and some cut to triangles for the corners, across 4-5 levels. Cut edges expose the striped honeycomb sandwich core. Tool-free slot-and-tab interlock, no hardware, glue, or graphics.

projects/job2110.label: Bernini expo stand
projects/job2110.represents: This is a "Sparkling Bernini" branded exhibition/expo stand (job2110) built by Picpong, photographed from five angles during a pre-event build/staging inside the fabrication warehouse. The photos show a complete modular booth: a long back wall, a freestanding portal/arch gateway with a branded header beam, a central square pillar/column, and matching freestanding counters/plinths. Bernini is a sparkling wine brand (the famous SB crown crest and "Sparkling Bernini" wordmark appear throughout).
projects/job2110.structures: A full modular cardboard/X-Board exhibition system. Key elements: (1) a long back/display wall built from multiple flat vertical panels with visible vertical seams between panel modules; (2) a freestanding portal/gateway arch made of two vertical leg-columns supporting a horizontal lintel/header beam that carries the "Bernini" crest and wordmark, creating a walk-through opening; (3) a tall square-section column/pillar standing free on the floor in the middle of the run; (4) two to three freestanding counters/plinths (podium/totem units) with a darker tan/beige top cap over a white body, each printed with the Bernini logo and used as reception or product-display stands. The pieces are clearly self-supporting flat-pack board construction (the columns and counters are hollow rectangular box forms), consistent with X-Board recycled cardboard. Panel joints, the box-beam header, and the rectangular plinth caps are all visible as build detail.
projects/job2110.branding: Heavy, consistent Bernini branding on every surface. The SB monogram crown crest (gold/red shield with "B", crown, ribbon banner reading "PREMIUM") appears repeatedly, paired with the script word "Sparkling" above the large black serif "BERNINI" wordmark. The same lockup is repeated on the back wall, the arch header beam, the central pillar, and all the counters. A faint ghosted/watermark version of the crest is also printed in pale tone on the left wall panel. Color palette is soft blush-pink/peach fading to white, with black typography and the multicolor crest.
projects/job2110.setting: Indoor, but NOT a live event floor — these are studio/staging shots inside Picpong's production warehouse before shipment. Background shows corrugated galvanized-metal warehouse walls, an exposed steel-truss roof with industrial strip fluorescent lighting, bare polished concrete floor, green emergency-exit "running man" signage, wooden pallets, a wall-mounted world map labeled "FUJIFILM," and printed safety/production notice boards and a job/status whiteboard. The stand sits on a light protective floor sheet.
projects/job2110.reuse: Strong portfolio/hero candidate. The five frames are sharp, well-lit, and clean, capturing the full assembled booth from several useful angles (full wide elevation, three-quarter, and the walk-through arch detail). Recommended use: Bernini_2.jpg or Bernini_4.jpg as the hero (full booth, balanced composition, clear branding), with the arch-detail shot (Bernini_1.jpg / Bernini_3-...jpg) as a secondary/thumbnail showing the gateway structure. Caveat: the industrial warehouse background (metal walls, exit signs, safety boards, pallets) reveals it as a staging shot rather than a live expo; for a polished web hero consider tight cropping or background cleanup, or pair with a true on-floor event photo if one exists.

projects/job1271.label: Yolo Photobooth
projects/job1271.represents: An "Yolo Photobooth" — a single enclosed photo-booth structure built by Picpong for MTN's "MTN Play As You Go" / YOLO campaign. The photos are clean studio shots of the same finished booth from multiple angles: an open-fronted phone-booth-style cabin with a green interior and a built-in seat, plus a couple of detail shots of the fully wrapped exterior panel. No people are present; these are product/build documentation shots, not live event candids.
projects/job1271.structures: A free-standing, room-height enclosed booth/cabin on a raised black plinth base. It reads as flat-panel modular board construction (X-Board/cardboard-style sheet panels with crisp folded edges and a slim profile) finished matte black on the frame and edges. Key built elements: a tall portrait entry opening flanked by two thick black-faced pillar/wall panels; a full top lid/roof panel capping the box; an interior fully lined in solid mid-green (back wall, side wall, ceiling) with a horizontal brushed-metal rail mounted across the top of the opening; and a rounded-corner green built-in bench/seat-and-counter unit (low cabinet with a soft radius) inside at one side. The exterior side and front panels are large printed wrap surfaces. Visible cut-outs/slots and small drilled holes in the front panel indicate mounting points for hardware (likely a screen/camera and signage fixings). Construction details visible: paneled corner seams, a vertical join line on the front face, the lift of the lid above the side walls, and the thick stepped base/plinth the whole unit sits on.
projects/job1271.branding: Heavy MTN branding. The MTN logo (black-and-white boxed wordmark and the small oval MTN icon) appears on multiple panels. Large stylised "YOLO" wordmark in outlined yellow/white capitals dominates the front and side panels. Tagline copy "How will you live to be remembered?" in dark type over the imagery. Social handles printed below the YOLO mark: Facebook "MTNPlayAsYouGo" and Twitter "@MTNPlayAsYouGo". Brand palette is MTN yellow plus black, set against a golden concert-crowd photographic background (silhouetted crowd with raised hands and stage lights). Interior accent color is a non-brand green.
projects/job1271.setting: Indoor, controlled white-cyclorama studio / workshop photo setup — plain white walls, white floor, even soft lighting and soft shadows. This is a fabrication/showcase shoot of the finished unit, not an on-floor event, expo, or mall installation.
projects/job1271.reuse: Strong reuse candidate for a portfolio thumbnail or gallery hero. The clean white-background studio shots are sharp, well-lit, and isolate the product clearly, which is ideal for a redesigned case-study card. The three-quarter angle (YOLO-Photobooth_8-copy.jpg or YOLO-Photobooth_3-copy.jpg) shows form, interior, and branding together and works best as the hero; the straight-on detail (YOLO-Photobooth_2.jpg) suits a thumbnail or branding close-up. No re-shoot needed — quality is good; only minor cleanup (consistent crop, possible background pure-white knockout) recommended. Note these are studio product shots, so they show the build but not the booth in live event use.

projects/gin-branded-wall.label: Gin Branded Wall
projects/gin-branded-wall.represents: A freestanding, self-supporting modular branded wall (an angled multi-panel display backdrop) built for Cape Town Gin & Spirits Company, photographed during fabrication/QC inside Picpong's workshop. The photos show the finished printed wall in different angled configurations, plus one shot with the print side facing away revealing bare white cardboard backs and triangular support feet.
projects/gin-branded-wall.structures: A large modular X-Board / corrugated-cardboard wall of multiple full-height vertical panels butted edge-to-edge into a long run that can be angled — a straight long wall in DSC_9032 and an L/zigzag corner configuration in DSC_9038 and DSC_9044. Each panel is supported from behind by integrated triangular A-frame cardboard feet that splay at floor level; no external metal frame or ballast. DSC_9048 shows the reverse: six bare white panels in a row, each with a triangular foot, a horizontal mid-rail/cross-brace, small numbered assembly labels (e.g. "22", "20"), and visible fold lines plus rivet/peg fasteners along edges indicating slot-together construction.
projects/gin-branded-wall.branding: Cape Town Gin & Spirits Company. Graphics repeat large photo-realistic gin bottles: "CAPE TOWN ROOIBOS RED GIN" (pink/red liquid) and "CAPE TOWN CLASSIC DRY GIN" (clear), each labeled "Small Batch / HANDCRAFTED" and "Distilled & Bottled in South Africa". Centered is the round brand logo "CAPE TOWN GIN & SPIRITS COMPANY" with a blue Table Mountain silhouette and tagline arc. The mural between bottles is a coastal Table Mountain scene across the bay with waves breaking over rocks (Bloubergstrand-style view). Palette is white background with blue ocean and pink/red and clear bottle accents.
projects/gin-branded-wall.setting: Indoor industrial workshop/warehouse — corrugated galvanized steel walls, exposed steel roof trusses and purlins, fluorescent strip lighting, polished concrete floor with painted yellow lane markings. Yellow forklifts, stacked pallets/cardboard stock, and small colored storage bins in the background. A production/staging space, not a live event floor.
projects/gin-branded-wall.reuse: Strong hero candidate. DSC_9044 is the cleanest, sharpest, best-composed shot of the full assembled wall in its angled configuration with even lighting — best for hero/thumbnail. DSC_9038 is a good secondary wide angle. DSC_9032 is usable but a person is partially cropped at the right edge and framing is tighter. DSC_9048 is valuable as a behind-the-scenes build/sustainability image showing all-cardboard construction and triangular feet, not a hero. All are well-exposed and in focus; no re-shoot needed, though the utilitarian warehouse backdrop could be cropped or masked.

projects/job1579.label: Mopanya Mall Bulkhead
projects/job1579.represents: This is the "Mopanya Mall Bulkhead" project: a set of large suspended ceiling bulkhead/banner panels installed under the glass-and-steel atrium roof of a shopping mall. The photos show the mall interior (atrium concourse, balconies, storefronts) and close-ups of the rectangular panels hung from the truss roof structure — apparently overhead signage/decor panels rather than floor-standing booths.
projects/job1579.structures: No cardboard/X-Board booths, counters, walls, gates, photo-walls or totems are visible. The only built deliverable shown is a series of large flat rectangular bulkhead panels suspended on cables/rods from the steel roof trusses (CTP8415-copy shows ~10+ deep-blue/navy and translucent silver panels staggered across the ridge; Mopanya_30 shows one large white/light bulkhead panel hung over the atrium on tensioned cables). These are overhead hanging panel structures, not stands or furniture. Background contains permanent mall fixtures only: glass balustrades with steel handrails, escalators, planter boxes with trees, tiled floor.
projects/job1579.branding: Minimal client branding on the Picpong panels themselves — they read as plain blue/silver/white surfaces. Visible branding is incidental mall tenant signage: a red neon "SILVER..." sign (likely Ster-Kinekor / Silverbird cinema) at right in CTP8404; in Mopanya_30 a red "GYM" banner upper right, "Sanlam" signage on a blue board mid-level, "KFC" red storefront, and a "DEBONAIRS PIZZA" outlet at right. None of these are Picpong client brands; they are surrounding retailers.
projects/job1579.setting: Indoor shopping mall / retail centre atrium. A multi-storey concourse under a large curved glass-and-steel barrel-vault skylight roof supported by exposed perforated steel trusses. Polished tile floor, glass-railed upper balconies, escalators, indoor trees in planters, and storefronts line the space. Workers in blue overalls are visible at floor level in Mopanya_30, indicating an active install/setup. Lighting is dim/evening interior with daylight through the skylight.
projects/job1579.reuse: Re-shoot recommended if booth/cardboard content is needed — these images do not depict any X-Board booth product and are weak portfolio pieces: dark, wide architectural shots where the actual deliverable (suspended panels) is small, low-contrast, and easily mistaken for permanent mall ceiling structure. CTP8415-copy is the strongest of the three (clean daylight, the staggered blue panels are clearly the hung product) and could work as a thumbnail or detail/texture image, but none are hero-quality. Mopanya_30 best documents scale and install context but is cluttered and dim.

projects/east-rand-mall.label: East Rand Mall
projects/east-rand-mall.represents: A custom cardboard "face-in-hole" selfie/photo-wall built by Picpong to celebrate East Rand Mall's 20th birthday. The three photos show the same free-standing double-sided board photographed from different angles inside the Picpong factory: one face (Pic_3) carries the "20 - My Kind of Place Since 1992" anniversary logo, and the other face (Pic_4, Pic_5) is the "Happy 20th birthday, East Rand Mall!" panel with three party-hat figures whose heads are die-cut as holes so people can poke their faces through for photos.
projects/east-rand-mall.structures: A single tall, free-standing self-supporting cardboard photo/selfie wall (face-cutout totem), roughly 1.8-2 m high. It is built from flat printed board panels joined at a right-angle: Pic_5 clearly shows an L/T-shaped configuration with a plain white panel acting as a perpendicular support fin/stabilizer behind the printed front face, letting it stand upright without external bracing. The board's cut top edge and laminated panel thickness are visible. The "people" side has three circular die-cut head holes (one shows the warehouse visible straight through it) for face-in-hole photo interaction. The whole unit sits on a flat white floor plinth/base board for display and photography.
projects/east-rand-mall.branding: East Rand Mall 20th-anniversary branding. The anniversary logo is a gold/bronze 3D "20" over a stylised red-and-grey arch/sunburst crest with the tagline strapline "MY KIND OF PLACE SINCE 1992". The party-graphics face reads "Happy 20th birthday, East Rand Mall!" in white type. Strong red brand background throughout, topped with a colourful multi-coloured balloon border (red, orange, yellow, green, blue, purple) and white line-drawn party figures with party hats, a birthday cake with candles, confetti and a cocktail glass.
projects/east-rand-mall.reuse: Good thumbnail / portfolio candidate, not a hero. The product (a polished, fully-printed double-sided face-cutout selfie wall) is appealing and shows Picpong's print quality and self-standing cardboard engineering well, but the backdrop is a cluttered factory floor with pallets, exit signs and a worker, which reads as "behind the scenes" rather than a finished event installation. Best use: a tight cropped thumbnail focused on the board itself (Pic_4 is the sharpest, most front-on and best-lit frame), or a "how it's made / our workshop" supporting image. For a polished hero, recommend a re-shoot of the wall in use at the mall with people's faces in the cutouts, or a clean studio/seamless background.

projects/bicon.label: bicon modular booth
projects/bicon.represents: A compact modular exhibition booth for the dental brand "bicon," photographed at an expo during/just after build. Two angles show the same corner stand: a tall dark totem column, a printed brand wall with values keywords, and two pedestal display counters on a green floor.
projects/bicon.structures: Multiple cardboard/X-Board structures: (1) a tall freestanding totem/column in dark teal-green carrying the white "bicon" logo vertically, with a capped flat top and visible screw/fastener holes down the edge plus small Hebrew text near the base; (2) a large flat printed back wall in cream/off-white with embossed faint inverted-triangle motif and dark teal value words (Fairness, Progress, Technology, Simplicity, Progress, Quality, Relevance, Unique, Reliability); (3) two matching pedestal/plinth display counters in dark teal-green, each with the white "bicon" logo and a lighter flat top surface (one shows a clear/glass topper); (4) white modular wall panels and corner pillars framing the booth; (5) a neighboring glass-and-aluminium display showcase/vitrine at right.
projects/bicon.branding: Dominant client branding is "bicon" (dental/oral-care brand) rendered in white lowercase logo on the dark teal totem and both pedestals, plus dark teal brand keywords on the cream wall. Neighboring booths show competitor/partner branding: a blue "meridol" + "GABA" panel with Hebrew copy and product bottle images (top-right of image 1), and a red "Colgate — YOUR PARTNER IN..." banner on the glass showcase (image 2). Small Hebrew text appears at the totem base.
projects/bicon.setting: Indoor exhibition / trade-show hall (dental or pharma expo). White modular panel walls, ceiling-mounted spotlights illuminating the brand wall, polished dark concrete aisle floor with a bright green carpeted booth floor. Image 2 shows build/setup in progress: bare aisle, cardboard boxes, packing debris, a discarded green bag, and an empty white table on the left.
projects/bicon.reuse: Usable as thumbnail/secondary gallery images rather than a hero. Image 1 (09.10.53) is the stronger, cleaner shot — booth fully dressed, lit, no clutter, logos legible — good for a project thumbnail or modular-booth case study. Image 2 (09.10.52) shows mid-build clutter (boxes, debris) so it is better as a "behind the scenes / construction" supporting image or should be cropped/retouched. Resolution is modest (~1024px, WhatsApp compression), so a re-shoot at higher resolution and straightened framing is recommended for any hero placement.

projects/job3219.label: X178 Zigzag Wall
projects/job3219.represents: A freestanding zigzag (concertina/accordion-folded) cardboard display wall built for Bellingham Wines, photographed from two angles in a studio. The panels promote Bellingham's wine ranges — "Ancient Earth," "Tree Series," and the "Insignia Series" / "The Bellingham Collection" — with large product shots of bottles and brand imagery.
projects/job3219.structures: A self-standing multi-panel folding wall made of flat printed X-Board cardboard panels hinged in a zigzag/concertina configuration (roughly 4-5 tall portrait panels that fold at angles so the wall stands on its own without external support). Each panel is full-height (taller than a person). The wall is stabilized by black angled foot/base supports at the bottom of each fold — visible as black wedge/L-shaped cardboard feet anchoring the panels to the floor. In the second image the raw cut cardboard edge and an exposed lighter inner panel are visible at the right, confirming flat-sheet board construction and a folded modular build. No counters, totems, arches, gates, or furniture — purely a folding presentation/photo wall.
projects/job3219.branding: Heavy Bellingham Wines branding. Panels read: "ancient earth — A WINE BY Bellingham" (gold filigree motif on black); "TREE SERIES — a wine by Bellingham" with an engraved tree illustration; the "Bellingham Insignia Series" postmark-style logo; bottle shots labeled Sauvignon Blanc, Shiraz, Merlot, Berry Bush Rose, Big Oak Red, Pear Tree White, Citrus Grove Chenin, and Mocha Java Merlot; a Cape-Dutch gabled homestead photo with mountains; a black-and-gold "The Bellingham. Collection — FOUNDED 1693" crest with the homestead emblem and URL www.bellinghamwines.com. A grey footer band across all panels states "Not for Sale to Persons Under the Age of 18."
projects/job3219.setting: Indoor studio / workshop space (not a live event). Plain white painted walls, exposed ceiling-mounted fluorescent strip light fixtures, and a pale laminate/plywood floor. The unit is staged alone for product-catalogue photography rather than installed on an expo floor.
projects/job3219.reuse: Good thumbnail and supporting gallery candidate. Both shots are clean, evenly lit, sharp, and color-accurate, clearly showcasing the zigzag folding-wall product and the print quality. The first (front-on) angle is the stronger hero/thumbnail because it shows the full zigzag silhouette and all brand panels; the second is a useful detail/second-angle. Limitation for a hero: it is a studio product shot on a bland white background with no event context or people, so it sells the product form but not the atmosphere — fine as a catalogue/product hero, re-shoot in a live setting only if lifestyle context is needed.

projects/job-9050.label: Maccabi conference
projects/job-9050.represents: Maccabi Healthcare Services (Maccabi/מכבי) 2019 professionals' health conference ("כנס מקצועות הבריאות 2019 – מובילים לשיאים חדשים"). The two photos show the main stage front: a large LED video wall displaying a "ברוכים הבאים" (Welcome) opening slide, with a row of freestanding 3D letters spelling "Maccabi" lined up across the stage edge in front of it. Image 2 is a tighter angled crop of the same setup as image 1.
projects/job-9050.structures: The standout fabricated element is a set of large freestanding 3D dimensional letters spelling "Maccabi" placed along the front lip of the stage. The letters are box/built-up letters with a glossy royal-blue face and white/light-grey returns (sides) and tops, each piece roughly knee-to-waist height and self-standing on the stage floor — a custom prop/sign build rather than a booth. No cardboard/X-Board booths, modular stands, counters, gates, photo-walls, arches or totems are visible. Other stage hardware seen is standard event production gear, not cardboard: a large modular LED video wall (panelized, fine pixel grid visible), a white lectern/podium at stage right, floor wash/LED uplights, a confidence/foldback monitor and microphone stands.
projects/job-9050.branding: Heavy Maccabi branding. Top of the LED slide shows the מכבי logo with the tagline "שירותי הבריאות הטובים בישראל." Center carries the conference title "כנס מקצועות הבריאות 2019 / מובילים לשיאים חדשים" beside a colorful circular "people holding hands" ring icon (red, orange, green, blue dots). Dominant large blue Hebrew headline "ברוכים הבאים." The 3D letters reinforce the English "Maccabi" wordmark in the brand's blue. Brand palette is blue/white with multicolor accent dots.
projects/job-9050.setting: Indoor conference hall / hotel ballroom, darkened for an event. The stage carries a wide LED screen as backdrop; the room has a dark ceiling with track/spot lighting and a patterned carpet (swirled hospitality-style ballroom carpet) visible in the foreground of image 1. Clearly a staged plenary/keynote room rather than an expo floor.
projects/job-9050.reuse: Re-shoot or use only as a low-priority thumbnail. These are dim, phone-shot stage photos with no cardboard/X-Board product visible — the only custom build is glossy 3D box letters, which is off-brand for a recycled-cardboard booth portfolio. Not suitable as a hero. If the Maccabi name is wanted for a client logo strip, a tighter crop of the lit screen logo could serve, but the photos themselves do not demonstrate Picpong's cardboard structures.

projects/electra-salon.label: Electra salon
projects/electra-salon.represents: Bar counter detail from the Electra salon event showing orange Electra logo standees beside bottles and a plant
projects/electra-salon.structures: No cardboard or X-Board structures are visible Only two small orange Electra logo standees on round white bases sit on the bar and these read as flat tabletop signage rather than a built cardboard structure The bar shelving espresso machine and lamps are the venue permanent fixtures
projects/electra-salon.branding: Electra brand mark appears as two orange interlocking EE logo cutouts on white bases A small EXIT or FIRE emergency sign is on the left wall No other client logos are legible
projects/electra-salon.setting: Indoor low light rustic bar or lounge interior used for an event reception with dark wood paneling a wine bottle shelf hanging stemware a brass espresso machine vintage pendant lamps and a wood bar counter
projects/electra-salon.reuse: Re shoot recommended for structure showcase The photo quality is high with sharp focus and warm bokeh so it works as an atmospheric thumbnail or supporting gallery image but it is not a hero candidate because no cardboard or X-Board build is shown

projects/activity-complexes-at-azrieli-m.label: Activity complexes at Azrieli Malls
projects/activity-complexes-at-azrieli-m.represents: Branded children's activity compounds installed on the open floor of Azrieli shopping malls. Image 1 is a Paw Patrol-themed "creative workshop" (סדנת יצירה) play area with kid-sized seating; image 2 is a Shimmer and Shine themed activity kiosk/play zone with an underwater/mermaid sub-theme.
projects/activity-complexes-at-azrieli-m.structures: Image 1: A full free-standing modular play compound built on an orange floor mat. Two tall vertical tower/totem panels (deep blue, printed with Hebrew vertical text) flank a large central printed backdrop wall showing Paw Patrol characters and a yellow banner reading "סדנת יצירה" (creativity workshop). Oversized die-cut character cutouts rise above the structure: a Marshall (fireman pup) figure mounted on a tall blue column at right, and a Chase cutout on a column at left. Inside sit clusters of small cube-shaped children's stools/seats and low hexagonal/cube tables, all printed with Paw Patrol paw-print and character graphics — clearly modular cardboard/X-Board furniture. Image 2: An L-shaped enclosed activity counter/kiosk made of large printed cardboard panels forming low walls about waist/chest height, sitting on an orange base mat. Tall corner totem columns carry vertical Hebrew signage; raised die-cut character cutouts (Paw Patrol pup, a clown/jester figure) project above the wall line. The right-hand wall is printed with an underwater scene (coral, fish, life-ring/buoy graphic). All surfaces are flat printed board panels assembled into counters and partition walls.
projects/activity-complexes-at-azrieli-m.branding: Image 1: Heavy Paw Patrol (Nickelodeon) licensed branding — Marshall, Chase and other pups, paw-print motifs. Hebrew text "סדנת יצירה" (creativity workshop) on a yellow banner plus vertical Hebrew text on the blue towers. Background mall retailers visible: H&M storefront (right), "twentyfourseven" sign, and an overhead banner with "שישי של שופינג" promo and an "Azrieli" (קניון עזריאלי) reference. Image 2: "פפ דורדורות" / Shimmer and Shine (Nickelodeon) branding with the two genie characters, plus a SpongeBob/Paw Patrol secondary panel and Hebrew signage; H&M and twentyfourseven storefronts again visible in background.
projects/activity-complexes-at-azrieli-m.setting: Indoor, on the polished stone/marble floor of an Azrieli shopping mall atrium. Image 1 shows a multi-level mall interior with an angular skylight ceiling, escalators, second-floor balconies and surrounding shops; a separate red kids' ride and a snack/candy stand are nearby. Image 2 is at ground level along a storefront strip with reflective glossy tile flooring and bright retail lighting.
projects/activity-complexes-at-azrieli-m.reuse: Image 1 is the stronger hero candidate: well-lit, sharp, wide angle that captures the entire branded compound (towers, backdrop, oversized cutouts, and matching cardboard furniture) in its mall context — ideal to showcase a full activity-compound build. Image 2 works well as a secondary/thumbnail showing color and craft of the printed panels but is a cropped landscape strip with less depth; usable as a gallery image. Neither appears to need a re-shoot — both are in good focus and good lighting.

projects/the-2018-annual-conference-for-.label: 2018 Annual Conference for Government Funded Religious Schools
projects/the-2018-annual-conference-for-.represents: An indoor exhibition installation for the 2018 Annual Conference for Government Funded Religious Schools, shot from an elevated angle. The photo shows a row of free-standing vertical display totems arranged in a colorful corridor, each carrying photographs of schoolchildren and Hebrew text, linked overhead by a lattice of horizontal beams — a modular pop-up museum/timeline-style exhibit.
projects/the-2018-annual-conference-for-.structures: A linear array of roughly eight to ten tall, monolith/obelisk-shaped totems (each tapering, wider at the base, with a flat top) standing in two facing rows. Each totem is a single bright solid color — red, magenta/purple, orange, lime green, teal, blue — and faced with mounted photo panels of children in classrooms plus large Hebrew display lettering. The totems are joined at the top by a grid of long horizontal blue beams/lintels forming a connected overhead framework (a beam-and-pillar lattice spanning the row). At the far end sits a blue totem topped with a Star of David cut-out finial, and a small flag-shaped blue topper appears on a totem near the front-left. The whole run sits on a printed blue floor runner/carpet covered in large white Hebrew words, defining the walkway. The lightweight, faceted, panel-built construction with flat color faces and beveled bases is consistent with cardboard/X-Board fabrication. Loose cabling/cords lie on the wood floor to the left, indicating it is an in-progress build/setup.
projects/the-2018-annual-conference-for-.branding: No Picpong logo visible. Branding is the client/event's: Hebrew display text on each totem and on the floor runner, photographs of school students, and a large panoramic photo-mural backdrop on the rear wall featuring an Israeli flag and historic black-and-white imagery. A red institutional emblem/logo appears on the window/wall at far left. Star of David motif used as a decorative topper.
projects/the-2018-annual-conference-for-.setting: Indoor, inside a large hall with a light wood/parquet floor, tall white structural columns, and a full-height glazed window wall on the left showing stone buildings outside (daylight). Stacks of blue folding chairs are parked along the left wall. Reads as a conference hall, auditorium foyer, or community/institutional building lobby being set up for the event.
projects/the-2018-annual-conference-for-.reuse: Usable as a portfolio thumbnail and as a secondary gallery image. Strengths: vivid full-color totems, clear demonstration of a connected modular cardboard exhibit system, good context. Weaknesses: phone/WhatsApp source (compressed, slightly soft), visible loose cables and stacked chairs reveal a mid-setup state, and the high oblique angle is documentary rather than polished. Recommend it as a thumbnail/supporting shot; for a hero image prefer a cleaner, eye-level re-shoot once the cabling is hidden and the space is finished.

projects/instagram-event.label: Instagram event
projects/instagram-event.represents: An Instagram-branded launch/influencer event inside an industrial venue, photographed from behind a long white catering/buffet counter. The frame shows the food-service zone in the foreground (with the Instagram camera glyph printed on the counter and a tabletop menu card) and a pink-walled lounge/stage area with guests and staff in the background.
projects/instagram-event.structures: The dominant structure is a long white buffet/serving counter with a thick slab top and a clean white modular panel base — consistent with painted X-Board/cardboard panel construction. Sitting on it is a freestanding white A-frame / tent-fold tabletop menu card with two angled foot tabs slotted into the base (a cardboard-style standee). Behind, a second long white modular table/bench and large white square planters holding palm/greenery, plus a low white plinth/seating block in the lounge. A tall white wall/pillar at right and a large pink backdrop wall define the space. Chrome retractable belt-barrier (queue) posts stand at left.
projects/instagram-event.branding: Strong Instagram branding: the multicolor (orange-to-pink-to-purple gradient) Instagram camera/lens icon printed prominently on the white counter top in the foreground, and a faint white Instagram glyph on the large pink backdrop wall behind. The menu standee carries Hebrew food-description copy (a mango/salad dish with Thai basil, coriander and roasted peanuts), confirming a localized Israeli event.
projects/instagram-event.setting: Indoor industrial/loft event space — exposed ceiling with ductwork, conduit, track/spot lighting and a hanging installation of bright yellow disc/lollipop shapes overhead. The room is styled into an event: pink feature wall, palms in planters, lounge seating, a catering buffet line, and clustered guests/staff (men in white and black polos). Daylight-feel, conference/launch-party setting.
projects/instagram-event.reuse: Thumbnail or secondary/detail image at best. Strengths: sharp, well-lit, and shows clear Instagram branding plus a clean white cardboard-style counter and menu standee. Weaknesses: it is shot over a catering buffet with random food trays, water bottles and a napkin pile in frame, the main subject is a tabletop and people's backs rather than a hero booth, and composition is cluttered. Good as a supporting/atmosphere shot or a crop of the branded counter/standee; recommend a re-shoot of the structures clean (no food, no people) if a hero is needed.

projects/family-day-israeli-air-force.label: Family day Israeli Air Force
projects/family-day-israeli-air-force.represents: An Israeli Air Force "Family Day" open-house event hosted by the unit/network designated "108 רשת הביטחון" (108 Security Network). The photos show a large freestanding "108" number sculpture used as a photo display/landmark, plus a matching branded entrance gate that crowds of visitors pass through into the event grounds.
projects/family-day-israeli-air-force.structures: The centerpiece is a giant freestanding "108" cut-out sculpture, roughly human-height, built as three separate flat numeral panels (1, 0, 8) standing upright on the paved ground — this is exactly the kind of die-cut X-Board/cardboard numeral structure Picpong produces. The "1" panel carries a blue sky/cloud graphic with unit crests/logos and the heading "רשת הביטחון"; the "0" and "8" panels are surfaced with photo-collage skins (soldiers, technicians, aircraft) plus a grid of circular badge icons with Hebrew sub-unit labels (e.g. רמ"ח, 502 יחידה, מבצעים, מודיעין) and a silhouette of a fighter jet. The numerals are self-supporting cut-outs with no visible external bracing. The second image shows a matching cube-shaped entrance gate/arch: blue-clad rectangular portal with a cloud-printed header beam reading "108 רשת הביטחון," a large circular winged unit emblem mounted on the left pillar, and a second round badge lower down — visitors walk through it. Both pieces share the same sky-blue printed cladding system. No counters, totems, or branded furniture beyond these display structures are clearly visible.
projects/family-day-israeli-air-force.branding: Israeli Air Force / IDF unit branding throughout. Repeated unit number "108" and Hebrew name "רשת הביטחון" (the Security Network). Multiple insignia: a winged circular emblem with an atom/electron symbol and a missile motif, the IDF sword-and-olive-branch crest, and a roundel-style winged badge. Sub-unit circular badges labelled in Hebrew. Color palette is consistently royal/sky blue with white clouds and white text.
projects/family-day-israeli-air-force.setting: Outdoor, daytime, on an Israeli Air Force base open area. Paved/tiled plaza underfoot, metal truss/scaffold structures and tensioned shade canopies overhead, blue sky. The second image shows a real fighter jet (appears to be an F-35/stealth aircraft nose) on static display above the gate, Israeli flags, and turquoise-and-white balloon arches — confirming a festive family open-house atmosphere with large crowds.
projects/family-day-israeli-air-force.reuse: Thumbnail / secondary gallery candidate. The giant "108" numeral sculpture in image one is the strongest, cleanest hero asset — it isolates a single dramatic Picpong-built structure with rich printed graphics. However both shots are casual phone snapshots (slightly soft, busy backgrounds of truss and crowds, no styling), so for a polished hero on the redesign a re-shoot or a tighter crop on the "108" sculpture is recommended. The gate image is heavily occluded by crowds, so it works better as supporting/context imagery than as a hero.

projects/our-idf-exhibition.label: Our IDF Exhibition
projects/our-idf-exhibition.represents: "Our IDF Exhibition" is a children's play/activity zone ("משחקי מוכנים" / games corner) installed inside a large public lobby, likely a military or aviation visitor center/museum. The photos literally show a grid of small white-and-orange kids' tables and matching stools laid out on a glossy white floor, with a decorated low wall behind them and a full-size military drone (UAV, tail number 828) displayed on the hall floor in the background.
projects/our-idf-exhibition.structures: The dominant cardboard/X-Board structures are modular flat-pack children's furniture: roughly a dozen low square tables and many matching stools, all built from thick white board with orange end-panels/legs and contoured cut-out leg profiles characteristic of slotted recycled cardboard construction. Each tabletop has a dark navy edge band and a printed white play surface (military/IDF iconography, aircraft, badges, a printed menorah/IDF emblem on the front table, plus simple game graphics). Behind them stands a long low printed partition wall/photo-wall with a light-blue and orange base, carrying the Hebrew title and flat cut-out cartoon character figures (a basketball player, two girls in headbands, etc.) and a tall cardboard column/totem to which a cartoon boy character is mounted high up. The build detail visible is the seamed board edges and the simple stool-and-table flat-pack joinery.
projects/our-idf-exhibition.branding: Hebrew headline text reads "משחקי" / "מוכנים" (games / ready) across the printed wall. Tabletops carry IDF-themed graphics including unit/branch badges, aircraft silhouettes, and a golden menorah emblem (IDF insignia) on the front table. A facility wayfinding sign reading "B2 / B1" hangs in the background. No Picpong logo is visible; branding is the client/event's own IDF and kids'-program identity.
projects/our-idf-exhibition.setting: Indoor, a very large modern public hall/lobby with floor-to-ceiling glass curtain walls on the left (showing parking, buses and trees outside), polished white flooring, exposed concrete columns and a curved upper balcony. A military UAV/drone is suspended-low/on a stand as a centerpiece exhibit, indicating an IDF or aviation exhibition/visitor center. Bright daylight floods the space.
projects/our-idf-exhibition.reuse: Usable as a secondary/thumbnail or gallery image rather than a hero. The two frames are near-duplicates of the same scene; quality is adequate (well-lit, sharp, casual phone/WhatsApp capture, low compression artifacts) but the composition is wide and cluttered with empty furniture and no people, so it reads as documentation rather than a polished marketing shot. Pick the cleaner first frame (-1) for a thumbnail; a re-shoot with kids using the furniture and a tighter angle on the printed tables/photo-wall would be needed for a hero. projects/service-with-a-mission.label: Service with a mission
projects/service-with-a-mission.represents: A row of branded exhibition wall panels and matching podium/counters set up for an Israeli national-service ("Sherut Im Shlichut" / "Service with a Mission") event, photographed in an indoor corridor. The photo literally shows a sequence of large printed backdrop walls with directional-arrow signpost graphics, each paired with a slim freestanding counter/totem in front of it.
projects/service-with-a-mission.structures: A series of large flat printed backdrop/photo-wall panels (the nearest one is a two-panel modular wall, roughly 2x2 m, with a blank white sign-frame area reading "בתי ילד") standing free on the floor. In front of the walls is a repeating line of four to five tall, narrow rectangular podium/counter totems with bright orange flat tops and blue printed bodies — consistent with X-Board cardboard freestanding counters/lecterns. The wall and counter graphics include illustrated colored directional road-signs/arrow shapes (blue, orange, green) suggesting a "pathways/choices" theme. No gates, arches, or furniture beyond the wall+counter units are visible.
projects/service-with-a-mission.branding: Hebrew event branding "שירות עם שליחות" (Service with a Mission) with a small star/compass logo, plus "חמד" (Hemed) logos top-left on each panel. Multiple partner/government crests and logos run across the tops of the panels, including what appears to be the State of Israel emblem, "חברה למשגב"/health-service-style logos and several small colored institutional logos. Decorative blue Star-of-David motifs are scattered across the backgrounds. Counters carry the same star logo and small logo strips. Predominant palette: navy/royal blue with white, accented by orange and green arrow graphics.
projects/service-with-a-mission.setting: Indoor venue — a corridor or concourse with a dark recessed/coffered ceiling, multiple round recessed downlights, and a smooth grey polished concrete or epoxy floor with faint colored line markings. The panels are lined up along one side against a glass/partition wall, reading like a registration or wayfinding area at a conference, expo, or institutional building lobby rather than an open expo floor.
projects/service-with-a-mission.reuse: Usable as a thumbnail or secondary gallery image. The shot is sharp, well-lit and clearly shows the modular wall-plus-counter system, which is good for demonstrating the booth product. However the strong receding perspective, busy mixed Hebrew/logo graphics, and empty corridor (no people, no activation) make it weaker as a hero image; for a polished hero a straight-on re-shoot of a single styled unit, or a populated event moment, would be preferable.

projects/israeli-air-force-event-in-hatz.label: Israeli Air Force event in Hatzerim
projects/israeli-air-force-event-in-hatz.represents: An outdoor exhibition for an Israeli Air Force (Heyl HaAvir) event at the Hatzerim airbase. The photo shows two large freestanding display walls printed with collages of IAF imagery — aircraft, helicopters, pilots and cadets in formation — set up on an open desert/tarmac area as part of a commemorative or ceremonial gathering.
projects/israeli-air-force-event-in-hatz.structures: Two tall, self-standing rectangular display panels / photo-walls, each roughly 2.5-3m high. Each is a flat full-color printed board (consistent with X-Board / cardboard panel construction) leaning slightly back, freestanding on the ground with no visible frame. The left wall is a single large panel with an angled, mosaic grid of photographs; the right wall is built from a 4-row x 3-column modular grid of separate printed tiles divided by blue diagonal stripes, suggesting assembled segments. No counters, gates, totems or furniture — only these two vertical signage/photo-wall structures.
projects/israeli-air-force-event-in-hatz.branding: Strong Israeli Air Force branding. Both panels carry the IAF winged emblem and the Hebrew wordmark "חיל האוויר" (Heyl HaAvir / Air Force) in a blue badge. Hebrew quotation text appears on both: left panel reads about building and operating force through victories and standing firm, professionalism and soldier culture; right panel reads "מורשת חיל האוויר היא מורשת מפוארת שנבנתה במו ידי הדורות המייסדים" (the Air Force heritage is a glorious heritage built by the hands of the founding generations). Imagery includes fighter jets, transport aircraft, Apache/transport helicopters, pilots, and historic black-and-white photos.
projects/israeli-air-force-event-in-hatz.setting: Outdoor, on a flat sandy/gravel tarmac at a desert airbase (Hatzerim). Bare sandy hills/berms in the background under a bright partly-cloudy blue sky, late-afternoon light. To the right, a row of portable toilets (orange, blue, yellow) and a person in a hi-vis yellow vest indicate an active event-setup ground. A small child stands between the two panels for scale.
projects/israeli-air-force-event-in-hatz.reuse: Usable as a portfolio thumbnail or secondary image: it clearly demonstrates large-format freestanding printed display walls in a real outdoor event. Quality is acceptable (sharp, good light) but it is a casual WhatsApp-resolution snapshot (1600px wide) with distracting clutter — portable toilets, equipment debris, and empty foreground. Not strong enough for a hero shot; recommend a cropped thumbnail focusing on the two panels, or a re-shoot/cleaner angle if a hero is needed.

projects/shikun-ve-binui.label: SHIKUN VE BINUI
projects/shikun-ve-binui.represents: A Shikun & Binui (Israeli construction/infrastructure company) display setup at a corporate or conference event, showcasing the company's green-building and infrastructure projects. The photo literally shows a row of five tall freestanding panel-boards lined up in an indoor lobby/foyer, each printed with a different project and a Hebrew headline.
projects/shikun-ve-binui.structures: A row of five tall, vertical freestanding display totems/panels (portrait-format flat boards, the kind Picpong fabricates from rigid X-Board cardboard). Each panel is a single flat sheet standing upright, supported from behind/below by thin white angled prop legs or feet visible at the base of several panels (two small kickstand-style legs per board). The panels have softly rounded corners. They are arranged in an overlapping staggered line, with three large foreground panels and at least two partially hidden panels behind them. No booth, counter, arch, gate or photo-wall — just the modular standing sign/totem panels. A separate white metal-and-plastic bar stool with chrome frame stands to the right (not part of the cardboard build).
projects/shikun-ve-binui.branding: Heavy "Shikun & Binui" (שיכון ובינוי) branding. Each panel carries the company logo at top — a stylized geometric cube/folded-square mark in blue and yellow next to the Hebrew wordmark "שיכון ובינוי". Brand palette is blue (top) transitioning to bright yellow/lime-green (bottom) on each board. Headlines in Hebrew per panel: "הכי ירוק שיש" (the greenest there is) with a rail/transit project and a "MetroTLV" sub-logo; "אנחנו בונים ירוק, ואתם מרוויחים!" (we build green, and you profit) over a coastal residential-towers render; "לראשונה בישראל: Greenroads" (first in Israel: Greenroads) over an aerial highway/agriculture image; "איך זה נראה?" (how does it look?) over a white residential development. Theme is green/sustainable building and infrastructure.
projects/shikun-ve-binui.setting: Indoor — a building lobby or conference-center foyer, shot at night/evening. Overhead the wall reads "FOYER" and large Hebrew signage; dark glass walls/doors at right show reflections and night exterior. Polished stone/tile floor, a staircase railing at left, glass balustrades. A person stands in the dark doorway at far right. Ambient warm interior lighting.
projects/shikun-ve-binui.reuse: Thumbnail / supporting gallery use at best, not a hero. The photo is sharp and well-lit on the panels with legible branding, but it is a casual WhatsApp-resolution snapshot (low-res, evening color cast, cluttered with a stray stool and a dark doorway). Cleanly demonstrates Picpong's modular standing-totem product and on-brand printing, so it works as a portfolio detail/thumbnail; a fresh re-shoot in daylight with a clean background would be better for any hero placement.

projects/joint-event-sponsored-by-the-ab.label: Joint Absorption Ministry / Rehovot event
projects/joint-event-sponsored-by-the-ab.represents: A multicultural community/integration event jointly hosted by the Rehovot Municipality and the Israeli Ministry of Absorption (Aliyah/immigration), staged in an indoor hall. The single photo shows guests milling among a row of branded display plinths topped with flower arrangements, with country-themed roll-up banners (Moldova, Ethiopia, Russia) and women in traditional Ethiopian dress at right.
projects/joint-event-sponsored-by-the-ab.structures: Three or four tall, rectangular freestanding pedestal/plinth columns (counter-height, roughly waist-to-chest tall) arranged in a cluster on the floor. Each is a clean white box with a colored top tray (blue and red) and is topped with a large mixed floral arrangement (yellow, blue, red, white blooms). These plinths are exactly the kind of lightweight modular column structure Picpong builds — they read as printed/wrapped board columns rather than heavy furniture. Behind them sit a series of pull-up/roll-up banner stands (vertical fabric/vinyl banners with watercolor-style graphics). Additional small white display pedestals at left-rear hold dark sculpture busts. No cardboard arches, gates, photo-walls, or booths are visible in this single frame.
projects/joint-event-sponsored-by-the-ab.branding: Each white plinth carries the Rehovot city logo and the Hebrew tagline "רחובות עיר המדע והתרבות" (Rehovot, City of Science and Culture) with the colored atom/pinwheel city emblem, plus a smaller secondary logo line at the base. The roll-up banners show national flags — Moldova, Ethiopia, and (far left) Russia — with multilingual welcome text: Russian ("Добро пожаловать на мероприятие…"), Amharic (Ethiopian script), and partial Hebrew/Russian. Top of banners carry small partner/sponsor logo strips (municipality + ministry crest), consistent with the joint Absorption Ministry / Rehovot branding.
projects/joint-event-sponsored-by-the-ab.setting: Indoor event, in a hall with a distinctly Mediterranean/Templer-style architecture: arched window recesses, cream plaster walls, a row of bare-bulb pendant lights on long cords, decorative patterned (Mediterranean cement-tile) floor borders inset into beige tiling, and a cafe/bar counter with stools and framed artwork along the right and rear walls. Reads as a cultural center or heritage-building lobby/gallery rather than an expo floor or mall.
projects/joint-event-sponsored-by-the-ab.reuse: Thumbnail or secondary gallery image only — not a hero. It is a candid, slightly dim phone snapshot (WhatsApp-sourced, 2018) with people mid-motion, busy background clutter, and the branded structures partially obscured by florals and guests. The plinth branding is legible enough to document the project but the framing and lighting are too informal for a polished hero. Recommend a re-shoot or a cleaner archival frame if these column structures are to be featured prominently.

projects/at-a-picpong-exhibition.label: At a PICPONG exhibition
projects/at-a-picpong-exhibition.represents: This is Picpong/X-Board's own exhibition booth at a trade show, photographed to showcase their cardboard fabrication capabilities. The photo shows a visitor (man from behind, in a blue shirt with a pink/magenta tote bag) standing in front of the company's branded stand, with a tall red Coca-Cola bottle-shaped display unit dominating the right side.
projects/at-a-picpong-exhibition.structures: Heavy use of X-Board recycled cardboard throughout. Back wall is a flat printed cardboard panel covered in raised/3D cut-out lettering reading "3 DESIGN", "+SUPPORT", "POS", "STAND OUT", "INSTALL", "+DIRECT PRINT", "EXPO", "FRIENDLY", "RECYCLABLE" with orange "+" cross motifs. Above it hangs a black suspended sign with a large white 3D "X-BOARD" wordmark (the X is a separate raised cardboard plus-shape). A matching white cardboard "X" totem sits on the right wall. Center foreground: a white cardboard table on a slatted A-frame base plus two low cardboard stools with curved tapered legs, all clearly made from layered/edge-visible corrugated board. Right side: a striking die-cut Coca-Cola contour-bottle display tower in red and white, built from stacked circular cardboard shelf decks (curved tiers) with a laser-cut "fizz/droplet" perforation pattern up the bottle neck; topped by a round red sign reading "Meals taste better with Coca-Cola." A dark cardboard reception/service counter with the Picpong fish logo and an X-Board mark sits mid-right. An orange-topped metal bar stool stands by the counter.
projects/at-a-picpong-exhibition.branding: Strong dual branding. Picpong's own product brand "X-BOARD" appears as the hero hanging sign and on the counter; the orange Picpong fish logo with "picpong" text is visible on the counter and printed on the stool seats. Coca-Cola branding is prominent on the right-hand bottle tower: the classic white Coca-Cola/"Cola" script on red, plus the slogan disc "Meals taste better with Coca-Cola." Wall copy doubles as marketing for X-Board features (DESIGN, SUPPORT, DIRECT PRINT, RECYCLABLE, etc.).
projects/at-a-picpong-exhibition.setting: Indoor trade-show / expo floor. The booth sits on a multicolor exhibition carpet (orange, mustard yellow, red/maroon zones) with a dark grey entry mat at the booth threshold. Overhead are clip-on spotlights mounted to the top rail of the modular booth wall, and an industrial exposed ceiling with ductwork is visible above. Neighboring booth partitions (white panels) are seen at the back.
projects/at-a-picpong-exhibition.reuse: Strong hero/portfolio candidate. The shot vividly demonstrates X-Board's full range in one frame: branded furniture (table, stools, counter), suspended signage, 3D cut lettering, and a complex die-cut client display (Coca-Cola bottle tower). Drawbacks: it is a casual handheld phone snapshot (2018 WhatsApp image, portrait orientation), slightly dim, with a visitor's back occluding the left third and a stray water bottle on the table. Recommend using as a supporting/thumbnail image or cropping tight to the Coca-Cola bottle tower for a clean product shot; for a primary hero, a re-shoot with even lighting, no people/clutter, and landscape framing would be better.

projects/ibm-conference.label: IBM conference
projects/ibm-conference.represents: An "IBM Alpha Zone" startup/partner showcase being set up under an event marquee. The photo literally shows a row of about a dozen identical small branded pedestal stands lined up in front of a living green plant wall, with a long two-tier reception/bar counter in the foreground, captured during build/staging (a man stands at left, a black equipment bag and a wheeled wooden crate sit nearby).
projects/ibm-conference.structures: Two main structure types. (1) A repeated row of ~12 modular pedestal/totem stands: each is a low rectangular box plinth wrapped with "IBM Alpha Zone" graphics (blue city-skyline artwork) with a thin twin-pole riser holding a white topper header sign — these read as lightweight modular/cardboard-style branded totems or plinth stands. (2) A large foreground reception counter: an L/straight bar with a white top surface, a lower white serving shelf/ledge, and bright blue side panels and end cheeks — a built branded counter consistent with panel-construction event furniture. A wheeled light-wood slatted crate/cabinet sits at far right.
projects/ibm-conference.branding: Heavy IBM branding: every pedestal front panel reads "IBM Alpha Zone" with a blue skyscraper-skyline graphic. The white topper signs carry individual partner/startup logos: ORPHEUS MEDICAL, change, INVOCU (approx), a "...OTSON" logo, AUTOMAT-IT, Jeeng, Timing, REPLIX, and more further down the row. Color scheme is IBM blue and white.
projects/ibm-conference.setting: Indoor-feeling but semi-outdoor: under a tensioned fabric marquee/tent ceiling with exposed truss, white pendant/dome lights and warm string lights, plus purple/teal uplighting. Floor is poured concrete with stone/wood-plank inlays; a tall lush living plant (green) wall forms the backdrop. Reads as an event venue terrace or tented expo space during setup.
projects/ibm-conference.reuse: Usable as a thumbnail/secondary gallery image rather than a hero. Quality is decent (sharp, well-lit, good color) but it is a candid setup shot: the foreground is dominated by the empty back of the reception counter, with a stray equipment bag, exposed cable, and a worker in frame. The strong repeated "IBM Alpha Zone" totem row is the asset's value; a tighter re-shoot of that branded pedestal lineup, fully staged and decluttered, would make a stronger hero.

projects/microsoft-winter-event.label: Microsoft Winter Event
projects/microsoft-winter-event.represents: A cozy winter-lodge / alpine "ski chalet" themed lounge installation built for a Microsoft event. The photo literally shows a styled seating vignette: two tan leather sofas facing a central coffee table, set in front of a faux fireplace with a stylized stag-head mounted above it, against a wood-plank wall.

projects/tat.reuse: Usable as a secondary / thumbnail or gallery image rather than a hero.
