Each totem carries a small emblem/crest plus a short Hebrew label (e.g., "מעיינותיך"). Subheads and section labels in smaller Hebrew text appear under the headlines. No Latin-script client brand or Picpong logo is visible. The visual identity uses watercolor/ink-wash backgrounds in blues and warm tones.
projects/hasidism-fair.setting: Indoor, a large institutional lobby or foyer - high ceiling with recessed circular downlights, multiple tall round structural columns, glossy reflective beige stone/marble tile floor, and a stone/brick feature wall in the background carrying large architectural mural graphics (Hebrew text and building imagery). A staircase is faintly visible at far left. The space reads as a conference hall, cultural center, or municipal building lobby rather than an expo floor.
projects/hasidism-fair.reuse: Usable as a thumbnail or secondary gallery image, not a hero. Strengths: clean, well-lit, shows the full modular panel-plus-totem system in context and conveys scale. Weaknesses for hero use: casual phone snapshot (2018 WhatsApp source), no people/activation energy, empty floor, slightly flat lighting, and the staggered angle leaves the leftmost units small and the foreground bare. Recommend a re-shoot (straight-on, populated, evening/daytime lit) if a hero is needed; otherwise good supporting evidence of a booth-exhibition build.

projects/super-heroes.label: Super Heroes
projects/super-heroes.represents: A "Super Heroes" event-activation / themed venue installation, photographed from below looking up at a large horizontal hanging sign. The photo literally shows a wide rectangular signboard reading "SUPERHEROES" suspended over an octagonal bar/counter, with two people working at the counter and a restaurant/lounge interior behind it.
projects/super-heroes.structures: The dominant structure is a large horizontal rectangular signboard/banner with a printed grey concrete-block (cinder-block wall) texture as its background. Across it the word "SUPERHEROES" is spelled in oversized 3D/decorative letters, each letter individually styled as a different superhero emblem (Superman's S, Batman's bat logo, Captain America's shield/star, Wonder Woman cues, Spider-Man web pattern on the second E, Iron Man's red/gold mask forming the final letter). A small Spider-Man figure is perched/climbing on the top edge of the sign at the right. Below and in front of the sign sits an octagonal (8-sided) bar or service counter with a slatted wood-look exterior and a dark countertop. There is no clearly identifiable raw X-Board / corrugated cardboard structure visible; the letters and board read as printed/painted panels rather than exposed cardboard.
projects/super-heroes.branding: Large title text "SUPERHEROES" rendered in mixed superhero-logo lettering (Superman, Batman, Captain America, Wonder Woman, Spider-Man, Iron Man iconography). No Picpong logo or other client/sponsor logo is visible in this frame; branding is entirely the Marvel/DC-style superhero theme.
projects/super-heroes.setting: Indoor venue — a dimly lit restaurant, bar or lounge. Visible ceiling detail includes exposed dark ceiling grid/ductwork with track lighting and warm spot/bulb fixtures, plus trailing artificial green foliage/vines draped across the top. Behind the counter are seating/dining areas with tables, brick-look walls and ambient warm lighting.
projects/super-heroes.reuse: Thumbnail / supporting gallery image at best. The shot is an atmospheric upward angle taken in low light, the colors are muted and the main sign is partly backlit and cut off at the edges, with workers and clutter in frame. It captures the themed concept well but is not clean or bright enough to serve as a polished hero image; recommend a straight-on, well-lit re-shoot of the sign if a hero asset is needed.

projects/google-cloud.label: Google cloud spring party
projects/google-cloud.represents: A photo/selfie backdrop wall built for the "Google Cloud Spring Party" corporate event. The image literally shows a freestanding white wall decorated with large 3D paper/cardboard flowers, butterflies, and flat printed stems/leaves, with the Google Cloud logo and "SPRING PARTY" headline centered on it.
projects/google-cloud.structures: A large freestanding modular photo/selfie wall, roughly 3x3 m, assembled from at least three vertical white panels with visible seams. Mounted on it are oversized three-dimensional flower sculptures clearly built from layered cut card/paper in a sculptural petal construction: a yellow dahlia and blue dahlia at left, a red rose center, a large blue dahlia upper-right, a green spiky dahlia/aster right, a yellow rose lower-right, and a big red peony top-right. Flat 2D printed/cut green stems and leaves run up from the base to connect the flowers, plus three flat cut butterflies (red, blue, tan). The petal layering and matte paper-stock texture are consistent with X-Board/cardboard craft. The wall stands on a green artificial-turf groundsheet against a dark backdrop.
projects/google-cloud.branding: Centered headline reads "Google Cloud" using the official multicolor Google "G" colors in the wordmark logo, followed by "SPRING PARTY" in grey uppercase. No other client logos or sponsor marks visible.
projects/google-cloud.setting: Indoor event setting, shot at night or in a darkened hall/venue. The wall is set against a black/dark wall or curtain, lit so the white panel glows, with a strip of green artificial grass laid in front as a floor/stage base.
projects/google-cloud.reuse: Strong hero candidate. The photo is sharp, well-lit, evenly composed and centered, with vivid colorful 3D flower craft against a clean white wall and dark background that makes the build pop. The visible panel seams and turf are minor but acceptable. Excellent for showcasing custom photo-wall / selfie-backdrop capability; usable full-bleed as a hero or cropped as a thumbnail without a re-shoot.

projects/gilat-summer-camp.label: Gilat summer camp
projects/gilat-summer-camp.represents: A photo-op / selfie installation from Gilat's "Gilat Summer Camp" employee family event. The shot shows two young boys posing inside a life-size cutout of a boat; one wears swim goggles on his head and holds a wooden oar across the bow, the other peeks over the gunwale, while a printed cartoon "captain" character in a white sailor's cap fills the far end of the boat.
projects/gilat-summer-camp.structures: A two-part standing photo-wall / backdrop: a tall flat printed sky-and-clouds wall behind, plus a free-standing die-cut boat front piece (the prop guests pose behind). The boat is a yellow/orange dinghy shape with two printed white-and-magenta life-ring (life-preserver) graphics on its hull and a printed sailor character built into the right side. These are flat printed board cutouts consistent with X-Board / cardboard photo-prop construction. A real wooden oar is used as a hand prop. No booth, counter, gate, totem or furniture in frame.
projects/gilat-summer-camp.branding: Top-left corner: black "Gilat" logo box with the satellite/swoosh mark and small TM. Top-right: "Employee Care" wordmark with a small purple/teal heart-leaf icon. Center headline graphic reads "GILAT SUMMER CAMP" in playful multicolor letters (navy, pink, orange, teal, yellow) with water-splash, beach umbrella, flip-flop, sunglasses and balloon illustrations. Bottom-center watermark "Maor Attias" (photographer credit).
projects/gilat-summer-camp.setting: Outdoor daytime event. Green grass/lawn is visible at the lower edges behind the boards, indicating the installation is set up on a field at an open-air summer family activation.
projects/gilat-summer-camp.reuse: Usable as a portfolio thumbnail or secondary gallery image showing a playful kids' photo-op build, but weak as a hero: the children's faces are prominent and would likely need consent/blur, the photographer watermark is visible, and the framing centers people rather than the structure. Best reused as a small thumbnail illustrating event-activation photo-walls; a clean re-shoot of the empty boat prop and backdrop would serve better for a hero showcasing the cardboard build.

projects/100-years-for-the-rabbinate.label: 100 years for the rabbinate
projects/100-years-for-the-rabbinate.represents: An exhibition installation marking "100 years for the rabbinate" (the centennial of the Chief Rabbinate of Israel). The photos show a freestanding, multi-panel display wall built as an L-shaped corner: one teal/navy panel carries the Hebrew headline "הרבנות" (The Rabbinate) plus a long body of explanatory text and a portrait of Rabbi Avraham Yitzhak HaCohen Kook, while the adjoining panels form a faux library backdrop with a presentation table and a framed-photo gallery.
projects/100-years-for-the-rabbinate.structures: A modular freestanding display wall, clearly assembled from flat board panels (visible vertical seams splitting the headline panel into two halves, and corner-mounting screws/grommets at the panel edges — consistent with X-Board/cardboard panel construction). The unit is configured as an L-shaped corner: a tall front wall printed dark teal with the large headline and rabbi portrait, returning to a side wall whose surface is a printed photographic mural of a packed sefarim (Hebrew religious book) library with full bookshelves. A small embedded screen/monitor is set into the headline panel showing a small framed B/W portrait (a "stamp"-style inset). In the HD view, in front of the library-mural wall sits a dark wooden presentation table (carved turned legs, antique style) stacked with rows of printed books/booklets and topped with a vintage oil lamp; behind it a large gold-framed B/W rabbi portrait and a salon-style cluster of ~10 small gold-framed black-and-white historical photographs mounted on the rightmost panel.
projects/100-years-for-the-rabbinate.branding: Dominant Hebrew branding: oversized cream/beige headline "הרבנות" (The Rabbinate). Body text is a quotation block in cream and gold type, attributed at the bottom to "אגרות הראיה / כרך ד / אשמד" (Igrot HaRe'ayah, vol. 4) — i.e. the writings of Rav Kook. Background ceiling banners in the venue read "ISRAELI EXHIBITION" / "...EXHIBITION" in English and Hebrew event signage. Color palette is teal/petrol-blue with cream and gold/amber typography. No Picpong logo visible; no commercial sponsor logos on the structure itself.
projects/100-years-for-the-rabbinate.setting: Indoor, inside a stone-clad public hall (Jerusalem-style cream limestone walls and pillars, polished beige tile floor). The first image shows the hall corner with stairs, hanging event banners, a wall-mounted book/shelf unit, and a directional sign reading "...JERUSALEM"; the HD image shows tall arched windows with daylight and greenery behind, confirming a large lobby or convention/exhibition hall.
projects/100-years-for-the-rabbinate.reuse: Usable as a thumbnail or secondary gallery image rather than a hero. The wide HD landscape shot is the stronger candidate — it best showcases Picpong's core offering (a complete printed modular booth/wall environment with integrated table staging) and reads as a finished, professionally lit exhibit. The portrait-orientation "-18" shot is tighter and shows panel seams/screws and some floor debris, making it better as a detail/construction or thumbnail image. A reshoot is not strictly needed, but a cleaner straight-on hero with the floor tidied would improve it.

projects/learning-spaces-in-schools.label: Learning spaces in schools
projects/learning-spaces-in-schools.represents: An immersive "learning spaces" installation built into a school corridor, where the walls are clad floor-to-ceiling in large-format printed graphics depicting natural habitats — a coral reef / ocean scene (with photographic dolphin, hammerhead shark, sea turtle, kingfisher, clownfish, lionfish and reef fish), a tropical beach lagoon, and a red sand-dune desert landscape. Speech-bubble info callouts in Arabic with sensory icons annotate the marine animals, turning the hallway into an interactive educational nature trail. The second image shows children walking and interacting with the wall graphics along a synthetic-grass path.
projects/learning-spaces-in-schools.structures: No freestanding cardboard booths, counters, totems or arches are visible. The build is wall-cladding / environmental graphics: full-height printed panel wraps applied across corridor walls, around a structural column corner, and onto existing doors (a blue door and an orange/desert door are wrapped to blend into the murals). Flat illustrated stylized trees and rolling-hill cutout shapes (green, teal, yellow) sit as foreground graphic layers over the photographic scenes. Image 2 shows the panels mounted along both corridor walls above a low painted skirting/dado rail, with a curving artificial-grass runner laid on the floor as a path element. No visible raw X-Board edges, but the panel-based wall system is consistent with Picpong's printed display work.
projects/learning-spaces-in-schools.branding: No Picpong logo or commercial client branding is visible. The only text consists of educational content in Arabic inside orange speech-bubble callouts, paired with round line-icons representing the five senses (hand/touch, eye/sight, ear/hearing, nose/smell, mouth/taste) plus additional pictograms. A vertical strip of these labeled sense-icons runs down the right edge of image 1 beside the wrapped desert door. Suggests an Arabic-language school, likely sensory/nature curriculum themed.
projects/learning-spaces-in-schools.setting: Indoor — an institutional school corridor/hallway. Visible cues: suspended acoustic-tile drop ceiling with recessed lighting and a vent, smooth pale vinyl/terrazzo flooring, a window with a painted rainbow at the corridor's far end, a hanging green plant, and standard interior doors with lever handles. Image 2 confirms scale with multiple children standing in the passage.
projects/learning-spaces-in-schools.reuse: Usable as a thumbnail and as a secondary gallery image for an "environmental graphics / themed learning spaces" category. Image 1 (1600x600 panorama) is sharp, well-lit and richly detailed — good hero-strip or section banner candidate due to its wide aspect ratio. Image 2 (1200x801) adds human scale and activity but is softer with motion and a slightly cluttered, dim mid-corridor; better as a supporting lifestyle shot than a hero. Neither shows raw cardboard construction, so for a page emphasizing X-Board build detail a re-shoot or a different project image would be needed; these best illustrate finished installed graphics rather than structural builds.

projects/carmel-winery.label: Carmel Winery
projects/carmel-winery.represents: A close-up table centerpiece at a seated event (likely a wine-themed dinner/gala associated with Carmel Winery). The photo shows a decorated dining table: a flat die-cut white scenic centerpiece standee depicting two figures carrying a large cluster of grapes (the classic "Spies of Canaan / Eshkol" grape-bearers motif tied to wine/Israel), flanked by two geometric brass/gold wire lanterns, one holding a lit white pillar candle, beside a low arrangement of white roses, lisianthus, baby's breath and eucalyptus greenery. Wine glasses are blurred in the background.
projects/carmel-winery.structures: No event booth, modular stand, wall, counter, gate, arch, totem or photo wall is visible. The only fabricated structure is a single flat die-cut standee centerpiece: a white panel laser/die-cut into the silhouette of two grape-bearers holding a grape cluster, with crown-like scalloped top edges. It appears to be thin rigid sheet stock (could be foam-board, acrylic, or cardboard/X-Board) standing freely on the white tablecloth as a decorative table sign/centerpiece. No structural cardboard furniture or build construction is shown.
projects/carmel-winery.branding: No Picpong client (Carmel Winery) logo or text is visible anywhere in frame. The die-cut figure is generic decor with no readable branding. The only branding present is the event vendor's watermark in the lower-left: "Michal Golan EVENT DESIGNER" in white script over a pink/magenta dress-form mannequin logo icon.
projects/carmel-winery.setting: Indoor, dim/warm-lit event hall or banquet venue. A formally set dining table with white linen, multiple clear stemmed wine glasses, upholstered chairs blurred behind. Soft warm ambient lighting consistent with an evening seated function, not an expo floor or trade-show booth.
projects/carmel-winery.reuse: Not suitable as a hero or primary thumbnail for a booth-builder portfolio: it shows table decor / floral styling, not an X-Board structure, and it carries a third-party event-designer watermark (Michal Golan) baked into the corner. Recommend excluding or, at most, using as a minor atmospheric/detail accent only after the watermark is cropped or licensing is confirmed. A re-shoot of actual cardboard structures would be needed for portfolio use.

projects/a-decade-for-assuta.label: A decade for Assuta
projects/a-decade-for-assuta.represents: The "A decade for Assuta" project: a 10-year anniversary decoration of the main entrance facade of an Assuta Medical Center hospital building. The photo literally shows the tall glass curtain-wall entrance covered with applied window graphics of colorful balloons and a large "10" anniversary mark, with a revolving door at center.
projects/a-decade-for-assuta.structures: No cardboard or X-Board built structures (no booths, counters, gates, photo-walls, or totems). The "build" here is large-format adhesive window-graphic / vinyl decal application across the multi-story glazed facade: a giant outline "10" logo, scattered blue, green, purple, silver and white balloon illustrations, and large Hebrew lettering ("אסותא") on the glass. Standing roll-up/pull-up banner stands flank the revolving door (left banner with green/blue graphic and Hebrew + "This is a no smoking hospital" text; a tall narrow blue totem banner on the right). Two black planters with manicured topiary shrubs sit either side of the central revolving glass door.
projects/a-decade-for-assuta.branding: Heavy Assuta hospital branding. The "10" anniversary logo in green/grey with Hebrew tagline ("שנים לרמת החייל / בית חולים..."). Large mirrored Hebrew wordmark "אסותא" (Assuta) across the glass. The revolving door header reads "ברוכים הבאים" (Welcome) flanked by "אסותא". Banner stands carry the Assuta logo and bilingual no-smoking-hospital messaging. Brand palette: green, blue, purple, silver/white balloons against glass.
projects/a-decade-for-assuta.setting: Outdoor, the exterior main entrance plaza of a modern multi-story hospital building. Reddish-orange stone/terracotta-clad walls frame a tall glass curtain wall (several stories of mullioned glazing). Paved stone/granite forecourt with a circular paving inlay leading to a central revolving glass door, automatic side glass doors, evening/dusk lighting with warm interior glow visible through the glass.
projects/a-decade-for-assuta.reuse: Thumbnail / supporting gallery image rather than hero. The photo is high quality (sharp, well-exposed dusk shot, strong symmetry), but it showcases facade window-graphics and banner stands rather than Picpong's signature cardboard/X-Board structures, so it has limited value for demonstrating the recycled-cardboard build capability. Good for a portfolio case-study tile illustrating large-format branding/installation breadth; no re-shoot needed.

projects/purim-event-for-dsp.label: Purim event for DSP
projects/purim-event-for-dsp.represents: A Purim event for DSP, styled as a rustic country/western (cowboy "wild west" / barn) themed photo set or decor backdrop. The photo shows a staged scenic vignette built from hay bales, wooden crates, and farm props arranged under a covered patio strung with festoon string lights.
projects/purim-event-for-dsp.structures: No cardboard or X-Board structures are visible. The build is entirely physical rustic props: a low horizontal fence/rail made of weathered wooden planks acts as a back wall; stacked natural wood crates form a tiered counter/display; multiple rectangular straw/hay bales line the front as seating/staging; a large red-painted spoked wooden wagon wheel leans center as the hero prop; wooden lanterns, a wine barrel, potted greenery, coiled rope, an acoustic guitar, cowboy hats, and faux cactus cutouts complete the set. There is one small square burlap/hay-textured board on the right bearing a horned-skull (cow skull) motif, used as themed signage/decor rather than a branded cardboard panel.
projects/purim-event-for-dsp.branding: No client/DSP logos, brand colors, or readable text are visible anywhere in the frame. The only graphic element is a decorative longhorn cow-skull symbol on the burlap board at right, which is thematic (western) rather than corporate branding.
projects/purim-event-for-dsp.setting: Indoor/covered outdoor patio at night under a vine-draped pergola roof, lit by warm festoon string lights, with a concrete floor strewn with straw.
projects/purim-event-for-dsp.reuse: Thumbnail or secondary gallery image at best. It is an atmospheric, well-lit lifestyle/mood shot that conveys event styling, but it contains no Picpong cardboard/X-Board product and no branding, so it has limited value as portfolio proof of fabrication work. Not suitable as a hero for a booth/structure showcase; consider a re-shoot or selecting frames that show actual built X-Board elements if this project included any.

projects/bat-mitzvah-for-omar.label: Bat Mitzvah for Omar
projects/bat-mitzvah-for-omar.represents: A travel/"around the world" themed Bat Mitzvah photo-backdrop installation for a girl named Omer (Omar). The photo shows a large printed selfie/photo wall depicting global landmarks and aircraft, fronted by a low staging platform holding freestanding 3D letters spelling "OMER" and decorative prop suitcases — a typical Picpong event-activation set built for guest photos.
projects/bat-mitzvah-for-omar.structures: Dominant element is a large modular photo/selfie wall, roughly 2.5-3m wide, made of three vertical flat panels butted together (vertical seams visible) — consistent with printed cardboard/X-Board panel construction, self-standing. A 3D model biplane (red) is mounted projecting off the top-right edge of the wall. In front sits a low rectangular stage/platform draped in dark navy fabric, on top of which stand large freestanding three-dimensional block letters "OMER" in glossy red. Flanking the platform are two prop "vintage suitcase" structures (red/yellow striped on left, light-blue striped with brown straps on right) used as decor cubes/props. A small flat cut-out red sports car/jet plane prop leans at front left; a straw sombrero and a small parcel/crate of props sit on the right.
projects/bat-mitzvah-for-omar.branding: Personalized event branding only — no Picpong logo visible. The backdrop banner reads "OMER" in Latin caps alongside Hebrew text "בת מצווה סובבת עולם" (roughly "Bat Mitzvah around the world"). The name "OMER" is repeated as the large red 3D floor letters. Illustration branding evokes world travel: Leaning Tower of Pisa, Eiffel Tower, Statue of Liberty, CN Tower, London Big Ben, red British telephone box, a cathedral, a commercial jet, biplanes, and hot-air balloons against a sky-blue sky.
projects/bat-mitzvah-for-omar.setting: Indoor, appears to be a covered patio, garage, or showroom/staging space. Right wall is exposed painted white/grey brick; left side has a dark doorway/black-framed opening and a tall shelving unit/bookcase partly behind the backdrop. Floor is polished grey concrete. Green foliage/garland drapes from the top edge of the frame, suggesting a semi-outdoor covered area. Daylight, neutral ambient lighting.
projects/bat-mitzvah-for-omar.reuse: Strong thumbnail and a good secondary hero candidate. The shot is well-lit, sharp, and cleanly composed with the full installation centered, showing the complete photo-wall plus props — clearly communicates Picpong's themed event-activation capability. Minor drawback: it is a staged/empty set (no guests or people for scale/life), and the surrounding garage-like background is a bit utilitarian. Usable as-is for a portfolio gallery tile; no re-shoot needed, though a populated event shot would make a stronger hero.

projects/leonardo-cosmetics-offices.label: Leonardo Cosmetics Offices
projects/leonardo-cosmetics-offices.represents: Interior wall décor at the Leonardo Cosmetics offices/clinic. The photo shows a cluster of five glossy printed campaign portraits mounted on a plain white wall, with a wheeled aesthetic/laser treatment device standing in the corner to the right.
projects/leonardo-cosmetics-offices.structures: No cardboard or X-Board structures are present. The visible build elements are five rigid printed panels (glossy acrylic or photo-mounted boards with a slight pale-teal/grey background tint) hung flush to the wall in a staggered, asymmetric gallery arrangement: one landscape panel of a shirtless man (upper left), a smaller square portrait of a woman below it, a vertical portrait of a fair-haired woman holding a product (top center), a wide landscape panel of a smiling dark-haired woman holding a product (center), and a tall vertical full-body panel of a woman in a black one-piece swimsuit (far right). No booths, counters, gates, totems, photo-walls, or branded furniture. The only freestanding object is a black-and-grey wheeled medical/aesthetic device (likely a laser or IPL machine with a handpiece and cable) positioned in the corner near the right-hand panel.
projects/leonardo-cosmetics-offices.branding: No legible Leonardo Cosmetics logo, wordmark, or color branding is visible. Each model holds a small product bottle/applicator but the labels are too small and reflective to read. A small dark logo/badge appears on the treatment device near its top, but it is not legible. No client text or color system can be confirmed from the pixels.
projects/leonardo-cosmetics-offices.setting: Indoor — a clean, brightly lit office or beauty/aesthetics clinic space with flat white walls and soft daylight. A wall corner is visible at the right edge. This is a permanent interior environment, not an expo floor or stage.
projects/leonardo-cosmetics-offices.reuse: Re-shoot recommended. The image is low resolution (~31 KB JPEG), the lighting is flat and washed out, the panels carry heavy glare/reflections, and the framing is tilted with the device awkwardly cropped at the corner. It documents the model-campaign wall décor but shows no Picpong cardboard/X-Board work, so it has little value as a portfolio hero or thumbnail for cardboard-build showcasing.

projects/hass.label: Hass Belgian waffle & French crepe stand
projects/hass.represents: A "Haas" Belgian-waffle & French-crepe catering stand built to mimic the front of a vintage VW-style camper van. HAAS-3.jpg shows the stand poolside (umbrella, surfboards, swimming pool with inflatables); top-haas.jpg is a wide crop showing two female staff serving from behind the counter, plus a tiled branded backdrop wall.
projects/hass.structures: The centerpiece is a faux camper-van service counter: a flat cut-out front of a classic split-window VW bus mounted to a serving counter, painted two-tone teal/turquoise blue with a white roofline and round headlamps. The countertop is light butcher-block wood; cabinet base below has handled doors and a round port/wheel cut-out. Flanking it are two upright wooden surfboards used as decorative side panels/totems (natural varnished wood with painted colored stripes). On the counter sits a row of small galvanized metal buckets/pails holding utensils or toppings. top-haas.jpg adds a repeating step-and-repeat photo/backdrop wall printed with the tiled "Haas" logo behind the staff. A wooden pergola/post frame and a large square white market umbrella shade the area. Note: this is themed/painted catering furniture and a printed backdrop, not visibly raw X-Board / cardboard construction.
projects/hass.branding: Heavy "Haas" branding. The backdrop wall is a step-and-repeat of a round/badge "Haas" logo featuring a stylized camper-van icon with small Hebrew tagline text beneath. The van counter itself reads as the Haas brand. One surfboard carries a small round blue logo sticker. Third-party product branding present: Nutella jars (with the red/white Nutella logo) displayed on the counter, plus jars of pink/red candy and sweets toppings.
projects/hass.setting: Outdoor private/garden event setting beside a swimming pool. Wooden deck flooring and artificial green lawn, lush tropical planting (palms, shrubs), a covered wooden cabana/structure with tiled roof in the background. Bright sunny daylight. Pool holds inflatable beach balls, a red flamingo/animal float and a ring float. A beach/surf summer-party theme throughout.
projects/hass.reuse: Good thumbnail/gallery candidate. HAAS-3.jpg is the stronger hero: well-lit, colorful, clearly shows the signature VW-van counter and surfboard totems with appealing poolside context. top-haas.jpg works as a secondary/detail shot — it documents the branded step-and-repeat backdrop and staff in action, but it is a low, very wide letterbox crop with a glare/seam down the middle and is less polished, so use it as supporting content rather than hero. No re-shoot strictly required; both are usable as-is.
